4	© 2026 KPMG ADVISORY, a French simplified joint stock company (société par actions simplifiée) and a member firm of the KPMG global

Context and objectives
CONTEXT
OBJECTIVES
• 	ABIVAX is a French clinical-stage biotechnology company developing innovative immune-modulating
therapies, with a focus on chronic inflammatory diseases.
• 	Headquartered in Paris, ABIVAX has a growing presence in the United States, where the VP Finance and
the project director are based.
• 	This dual footprint drives ERP requirements including multi-currency/multi-GAAP capabilities, SOX 2
compliance, and bilingual support (French/English).
• 	ABIVAX has selected a new ERP to support its growth and reduce time spent on operational/manual
activities.
• 	A key operational challenge is the decentralized Purchase-to-Pay (P2P) process managed by all
employees, meaning the new ERP will significantly change day-to-day habits and require strong user
adoption.
• 	Secure the ERP deployment end-to-end, ensuring the solution is fit-for-purpose, covers ABIVAX’s
operational needs, and reflects its specific context (France/U.S. footprint, multi-currency, bilingual usage,
and SOX 2 expectations).
• 	Protect the project timeline and key milestones to achieve a Go-Live on January 1, 2027, with a controlled
cutover, clear ownership, and proactive risk/issue management.
• 	Drive fast and sustainable user adoption, through change management, role-based training, and hypercare,
so employees can use the new tool confidently and efficiently as quickly as possible.

5	© 2026 KPMG ADVISORY, a French simplified joint stock company (société par actions simplifiée) and a member firm of the KPMG global
organization of independent member firms affiliated with KPMG International Limited, a Private English company limited by guarantee. All

3 key challenges to make your ERP project a success
ERP aligned with business needs
Ensure the selected ERP solution adequately
covers business and technical needs, controls
implementation complexity, and is configured to
Abivax’s operational context and compliance
requirements.
User Adoption & Change Management
Drive adoption and user proficiency for the future
ERP, particularly across procurement / P2P users,
given the significant changes versus current
processes and tools.
Dual Footprint Alignment & Vendor
Control
Ensure day-to-day alignment between a US-based
Transformation Director and Europe-based
business and integrator teams, while providing
strong vendor management and interface
coordination to keep delivery on track.

6	© 2026 KPMG ADVISORY, a French simplified joint stock company (société par actions simplifiée) and a member firm of the KPMG global

We propose a structured approach in 3 key areas to support the
implementation
1 – FUNCTIONAL ASSISTANCE
• 	Ensure that the needs expressed are considered in
the target solution
• 	Secure processes and solution through controls
integration and SoD compliance by design
• 	Initialize the target system with compliant data
• 	Validate solution parameters and interfaces with
third-party solutions
• 	Prepare and orchestrate solution go live
• 	Organize post-go live support
2 – CHANGE MANAGEMENT
• 	Build clear project value case and design a tailored
change approach and governance
• 	Understand key roles impacted and identify changes
& associated change required activities
• 	Build Communication and Engagement Plan and
associated key messages
• 	Identify role-based training needs and build training
curriculum	
OBJECTIVES
3 – PROJECT MANAGEMENT
 	Mobilize teams and remind them the priorities
 	Monitor compliance with deadlines, scope, quality of
deliverables, budget and resources
 	Ensure risk control
 	Prepare and orchestrate solution Go-Live
1. 	POWERED Methodology – Enhanced with AI
KPMG Powered delivers a 360° framework to secure ERP programs across all phases—from scope definition through deployment—now supported by
AI at key steps to improve speed and reliability :
• 	Conduct precise analysis on Day-to-day
workshops to identify and refine business
needs
• 	DevOps setup and governance to support the
development stream
• 	UAT creation and orchestration (planning,
coordination …)

Our convictions for what it takes to lead successfully the deployment and
adoption of your solution
І 	Go beyond the usage of the system only
by understanding and integrating the
emotional and rational dimension of
change
І 	Ensure all teams understand the
processes and the new role they will have
to play on top of the how to navigate the
system itself
І 	Ensure coordination and engagement
between US and French teams to reach
common success
І 	Empower operational and finance teams
at each steps of the process and are fully
clear on what they should be doing or not
І 	Address ABIVAX’s specific operational
and cultural challenges
WHY DO WE BELIEVE CHANGE MANAGEMENT IS
KEY TO YOUR PROJECT SUCCESS
WHAT WILL BE KEY TO ENSURE AN END-TO-END ADOPTION
SHIFTING MINDSETS:
Through an analysis by
role/persona of emotional and
rational reactions
BUILDING COMPETENCIES:
“In the flow of work” training, based on day-to-day
scenarios, to succeed in front of tomorrow’s
challenges (CSRD, data, etc.)
ENGAGING EXPERIENCE:
Specific adoption actions by role, to
maintain interest, engagement and
implications of your teams in the project
VALUE REALIZATION:
On-shelf, pre-built
assets to avoid starting
from 0 and focus only
on your specific
challenges, risks and
opportunities, and
maximize value
realization
ENSURING
UNDERSTANDING:
With targeted and
engaging
communications

each (incl. video demos for key steps in the
system)
Our detailed change activities and deliverables
Based on your initial needs, we have selected a list of activities and deliverables that we believe will be critical to secure your upcoming deployment as part
of the Vision & Validate phases of the project.
In addition, we have identified complementary activities/deliverables that would support further your team in reinforcing adoption approach and content.

Governance
Steering Committee (every 2 months)
The strategic committee enables us to carry out a strategic review of the application over a 2-month
period, and to ensure project direction.
Design Authority Board (monthly, on design phase only)
The design authority board is essential to ensure transversal communication and decision-making
regarding business topics during the design phase. It could last after design phase in case of a scope
change for example.
Project Committee (weekly with the BPO)
The project committee provides a frequent review of services in progress, to ensure that projects are
well managed and progressing smoothly.
01
02
03
To ensure coherent and efficient management of the new ERP implementation, a standard three-level governance structure will be put in
place. The aim is to mobilize the appropriate resources to make decisions at each level and escalate arbitration points.

as a long-term partner, he works closely with clients to scope and lead the implementation of complex transformations: digital strategy, IT master plans, business IT
transformation (sales & omnichannel CRM, Industry 4.0), and new IS/IT operating models.
He leads the development of architecture capabilities at KPMG France, contributing to the scoping and implementation phases of digital transformation or IT
modernization initiatives.

Profile
Olivia joined KPMG after a first experience in consulting at RSM. She has 3 years of professional experience in IT consulting, particularly in SAP. She has worked
on several advisory projects helping clients manage their technological risks, mainly in the context of internal control.
Professional experience
Internal Control and Internal Audit in Projects (SAP S/4, COUPA, EBX TIBCO & other ERPs)
Olivia assisted an international group in the cosmetics industry in the redesign of an SAP S/4 HANA Core Model, and specifically contributed to:
• 	The design of a new risk and control framework for the group, co-developed with the business, internal control, and IT stakeholders, to address the three levels
of control (“by design” controls) for P2P, R2R, O2C and F2S processes.
• 	The implementation of new roles, “SoD compliant by design.”
• 	Supporting internal audit to review the audit methodology, creating a dedicated Internal Auditor role in the tools, and designing specific reports for internal audit.
• 	Creating training content to support change management and end-user adoption.
• 	Producing key documentation for onboarding new members of the Group’s internal control team and for the deployment of the project in internal control areas.
• 	Documenting a global view of the project and the tasks of each stakeholder at the Group level, including knowledge transfer documentation and the “IC
Playbook” detailing the deployment of the project (roles involved and steps to follow for successful completion of internal control objectives).
• 	Supporting the “move to Run” phase by creating a RACI matrix to ensure continuous improvement of the project and its long-term adoption by end users.
Olivia also assisted a large international energy group with the implementation of automated detective controls during the deployment of the Highbond tool. She
contributed to:
• 	The creation of automated controls using ACL, implemented within the tool and programmed based on a new risk and control framework for the group, co-
developed with the business, internal control, and IT stakeholders, to address the three levels of control (“by design” controls).
• 	Producing training content to support change management and leading certain training sessions (in both French and English).
Key expertise and sector
• 	Key Skills: Bilingual in English/French, excellent communication and client relationship skills, highly organized.
• 	Cross-functional IT Skills: SAP ECC6, SAP S/4 Hana, SOX compliance
• 	Sectors: Energy, Cosmetics Industry.
